assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'ALL 4 slices will restart simultaneously' and 'this is not a rolling update', clearly explaining atomic recreation behavior

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'any maintenance event affecting one slice triggers restart of the entire multislice environment' and 'Even if maintenance is scheduled for just one slice, all connected slices restart'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Your training job will be completely terminated' and 'Complete Training Interruption' with details about simultaneous restart

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response specifically addresses this with 'If insufficient TPU v5e capacity exists during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available - this could be hours or longer'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Force Checkpoint Before Maintenance' section with specific commands and recommends '72 hours before: Force checkpoint' in timeline

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response provides restart automation scripts, recovery planning section, and restart command template with 'RESUME_FROM_CHECKPOINT' configuration

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer maintenance events - this option is not discussed

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Maintenance Window Strategy' section with Option A recommending controlled restart and scheduling around training breaks
